name: TextField
summary: Single- or multi-line text input with `label`/`helperText`/`error`+`errorText`, `size`, and a `width` model; controlled via `value` + `onChange` or auto-bound to a `FormControl` by `name`. Colours follow the theme intent contract (consistent with Button/Checkbox) and it fills its container's width by default. The default for free-form text entry.

props.name: name
props.description:
Field identifier used for FormControl binding and forwarded to the underlying control
when applicable. Components can require `name` locally if they depend on it.

props.name: label
props.description: Visual label rendered by the component or its wrapper. Accepts string or node.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Supplemental hint or validation content typically rendered under the control.

props.name: error
props.description: Marks the field invalid; components may adjust visuals and helper text color.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Stretches the field wrapper to occupy the full available width.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

props.name: width
props.description:
Explicit field width — number → px, or any CSS length. Overrides the
default `width:100%`.

props.name: errorText
props.description:
Validation message shown in the role='alert' region when `error` (or a
matching FormControl error). Falls back to `helperText`.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Canonical value change event (fires on each input).

bestPractices: Always provide a clear `label` (rendered visibly and associated to the input); with no `label`/aria-label/aria-labelledby it dev-warns. Avoid placeholder-only fields.

It fills its container's width by default (no more ~20ch UA default) and shrinks correctly in a flex/grid row. Set an explicit `width` (number → px, or any CSS length e.g. `'40ch'`) to constrain it; `fullWidth` makes it grow to consume slack in a row.
Choose the correct `type` (e.g., `email`, `password`, `number`) and set `autoComplete` to improve UX and a11y.

Pick a control model deliberately: `value` for app-state control, `defaultValue` for simple local inputs. Precedence is explicit `value` prop > FormControl binding > internal state, latched at mount — passing both a `value` prop and a form `name` dev-warns (the prop wins for rendering; edits still write through).

In forms, set a stable `name` and seed every bound key in `createFormStore(initial)`. A `<FormControl disabled>` disables the field, and a name-keyed `errors` map on the FormControl shows the error here (the field's own `error`/`errorText` still win).

Convey validation with `error` + `errorText` (or `helperText` as the fallback message): the message renders in a `role='alert'` region and the field is marked `aria-invalid`. Neutral `helperText` is NOT announced (no aria-live); reserve announcement for actual errors.

Prefer the canonical `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` over raw `onChange`: each `ChangeInfo.source` is honest — typing `keyboard`, paste/drop `clipboard`, programmatic writes `programmatic`; Enter commits `keyboard`, blur commits `programmatic`. Caller `onBlur`/`onKeyDown` are composed (no longer dropped).
Pick `size` (`xs`–`md`–`xl`) for the field height/text — md matches the Button height so an input and a Button line up in a row. On coarse pointers the input height floors at ≥44px automatically.
Use `as='textarea'` with `rows` for multi-line input. Prefer theme tokens and `preset` over ad-hoc CSS. (`variant` accepts `outlined`/`filled`/`underline`; only `outlined` is implemented for 1.0.)

examples.title: Email + Password
examples.code:
<Stack gap={1}>
  <TextField name='email' type='email' label='Email' placeholder='you@example.com' autoComplete='email' />
  <TextField name='password' type='password' label='Password' autoComplete='new-password' />
</Stack>

examples.title: Multiline with helper
examples.code:
<Stack gap={1}>
  <TextField name='bio' as='textarea' rows={4} label='Bio' placeholder='Tell us about yourself' helperText='Use 2–3 sentences.' />
</Stack>

examples.title: Validation: error + errorText (role=alert)
Explicit width in a row
examples.code:
<Stack direction='row' gap={1} align='end'>
  <TextField name='code' label='Code' width='8ch' />
  <Button>Apply</Button>
</Stack>
